nameMeanings.name: de
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Dilip, Anirban, Gopal, Alok, Anindya, Arunava, Asit, Asok, Dibyendu, Goutam, Indra, Indranil.

variants.name: Dey
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Ashit, Indu, Naina, Partha, Soumya, Ajit, Anjan, Anjana, Anup, Arjun, Arun, Ashim.
English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

nameMeanings.name: Mennon
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Irish and Scottish: shortened form of   McKennon  .

variants.name: Kenon
meanings: Probably an altered form of   Kennon  .

variants.name: McKennon
meanings: Irish: Anglicized form of Gaelic Mac Fhionnáin, a patronymic from the personal name Fionnán, a personal name representing a diminutive of fionn ‘white’. The name is now usually shortened to Kennon and there is possible confusion with Scottish McKinnon .

nameMeanings.name: Sybil
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: From the name (Greek Sibylla or Sybilla, with confusion over the vowels from an early period) of a class of ancient prophetesses inspired by Apollo. According to medieval theology, they were pagans denied the knowledge of Christ but blessed by God with some insight into things to come and accordingly admitted to heaven. It was thus regarded as a respectable name to be borne by Christians. This name was introduced to England by the Normans and enjoyed considerable popularity in the Middle Ages.

variants.name: Sybilla

historicEvents.title: 100 Years of Conflict Between England and France Ends
historicEvents.description: Treaty of Paris was signed on December 4, 1259, which ended 100 years of conflict between England and France.

en.lifeSummary: Sybil de Mennon was born in 1230, in Great Musgrave, Westmorland, England. She married Thomas de Musgrave in 1255, in Great Musgrave, Westmorland, England. They were the parents of at least 2 daughters. She died in 1287, in Great Musgrave, Westmorland, England, United Kingdom, at the age of 57.

ko.lifeSummary: Sybil de Mennon(은)는 1230년 영국 웨스트모를랜드 그리트 무스그레이브에서 태어났다. 그녀는 1255년 영국 웨스트모를랜드 그리트 무스그레이브에서 Thomas de Musgrave(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 두 명의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 1287년 영국 잉글랜드 웨스트모를랜드 그리트 무스그레이브에서 57세의 나이로 사망했다.
